Source_Species.reason: The study was performed in Arabidopsis thaliana and JAZ5 mutants were analyzed in this species.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is explicitly the study species.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper states Col-0 for many mutant analyses, but JAZ5 source variety is not explicitly stated as a field value for the gene itself in Results.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments involving JAZ5 were done in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is explicitly the experimental species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: JAZ5/jaz5/10 phenotypes are compared with wild-type Col-0 in the Results.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: JAZ5 is linked in Results to jasmonic acid dynamics and coronatine-mediated phenotypes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: JA is treated as a jasmonate/oxylipin phytohormone and COR as a phytotoxin/hormone mimic throughout the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly discusses JA in defense/hormone signaling and COR in promoting virulence and chlorosis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Hormones and COR were measured in infected leaves/foliar tissue.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show jaz5/10, but not jaz5 alone, has enhanced COR-mediated chlorosis, modestly increased susceptibility, and altered JA/SA/ABA levels.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized points are supported: JAZ5 specifically co-operates with JAZ10, the double mutant shows strongest phenotype, and JAZ5 induction is partly COR dependent and partly COR independent.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all described and used in the Results for JAZ5/jaz5/10: reverse genetics, infection phenotyping, growth assays, hormone profiling, qRT-PCR, COR assays, root assays, and mRNA-seq.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Microarray/qRT-PCR data show rapid induction by DC3000 around 6 hpi, JAZ5 maximal around 8 hpi, early induction with COR-deficient bacteria, and later partial COR dependence with need for additional bacterial signals.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: This accurately summarizes leaf infection expression behavior reported for JAZ5.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization of JAZ5 is reported in this paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: JAZ5 is a JAZ repressor; Discussion explicitly notes JAZ5 has two EAR domains and lacks the highly conserved LPIARR motif found in many JAZ proteins.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper supports JAZ5 interaction with MYC2 and discusses interactions with TOPLESS and NINJA from cited work, but direct JAZ5-JAZ10 interaction is not shown in this paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These treatments were explicitly used for JAZ5/jaz5/10 analyses.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of JAZ5 together with JAZ10 caused enhanced COR sensitivity/chlorosis and modestly enhanced susceptibility.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper clearly shows the decisive phenotype requires combinatorial loss with JAZ10 and that COR alone gives only transient JAZ5 induction.

Omics_Data.reason: The CATMA microarray GEO accession GSE56094 and mRNA-seq GEO submission GSE72461 are explicitly given.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable inference from the data but no breeding/engineering application is proposed or tested in the paper.

corrections.reason: No subcellular localization of JAZ5 is reported in this paper.

Source_Species.reason: JAZ10 was studied in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: JAZ10 phenotypes are largely in Col-0 comparisons, but the source variety for the gene field is not explicitly stated as such.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the experimental species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: JAZ10/jaz5/10 analyses are reported against Col-0.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: JAZ10 is linked to JA dynamics and COR-mediated phenotypes in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: JA and COR classes/functions are consistent with how they are described in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly frames JA as defense signaling and COR as a virulence-promoting chlorosis-inducing mimic.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support collaboration with JAZ5 in restricting COR cytotoxicity, restricting bacterial growth in some assays, and modulating JA accumulation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported: decisive partnership with JAZ5, mild single-mutant phenotypes with increased growth at high inoculum, and sustained infection-induced expression with JAZ10.3 accumulation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These approaches were used for JAZ10/jaz5/10 in Results, including splice-variant qRT-PCR and mRNA-seq.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper shows rapid induction near 6 hpi, sustained accumulation with peak at 16 hpi, early COR-independent induction, and strong later COR/COI1 dependence; JAZ10.3 accumulates from 8 hpi.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Consistent with the leaf infection time-course data.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: This paper does not report subcellular localization of JAZ10.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: JAZ10 is treated as a JAZ repressor and alternatively spliced; the paper explicitly states JAZ10.3 is predicted to bind COI1 but not be efficiently ubiquitinated.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper states JAZ10 interacts with MYC2 in yeast two-hybrid, but direct JAZ10-JAZ5 interaction is not demonstrated here.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Loss of JAZ10 contributes to increased symptoms and increased bacterial growth, particularly in combination with jaz5 and at high inoculum for jaz10 single mutants.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly attributes sustained JAZ10 increase largely to JAZ10.3 and discusses its predicted signaling attenuation role.

Omics_Data.reason: GSE56094 and GSE72461 are explicitly provided.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an extrapolated application not directly proposed or tested in the paper.

corrections.reason: This paper does not report subcellular localization of JAZ10.

Source_Species.reason: COI1 was studied via the Arabidopsis coi1-16 mutant.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The Results explicitly describe the JA receptor mutant coi1-16 in a Col-5 background context.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the experimental species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Hormone profiling compares wild-type Col-5 and coi1-16.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: COI1 is directly analyzed with respect to JA, SA, and COR-associated infection responses.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These metabolite classes are consistent with the paper's descriptions.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly discusses JA/SA defense signaling and COR as a JA mimic promoting virulence.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Hormone measurements were in infected leaves/foliar tissue.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show COI1 is required for much of COR-responsive JAZ10 expression and affects JA/SA dynamics, while much late JA accumulation in COR-deficient infection is COI1 independent.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by the Results and discussion of Fig. 1 and Fig. 7.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Mutant analysis, hormone profiling, and qRT-PCR time-course are all used; pathogen infection assays are also part of the study context.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: COI1 is treated mainly as a receptor tested genetically rather than by its own expression profiling.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Subcellular localization of COI1 is not reported in this paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly refers to COI1 as the JA receptor; while the full F-box/SCF description is background knowledge, it is not stated in Results, so support is partial but acceptable as standard identity.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper directly states JAZ10.3 is predicted to bind to COI1 and discusses COI1-dependent JAZ regulation; JAZ proteins broadly are implicated as COI1 partners.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: COI1 was tested under DC3000 and COR-deficient bacterial challenge.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper states coi1-16 is compromised in most jasmonate responses, implying broad jasmonate-response liabilities.

Other_Important_Info.reason: COI1 receptor genetics were central to interpreting hormone dynamics and JAZ induction, even though the main focus was JAZ5/JAZ10.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible inference, but no engineering strategy is tested or proposed in the paper.

corrections.reason: Subcellular localization of COI1 is not reported in this paper.
